name: Siteware Email Automation — Lane 1 (IMAP → Triage → Route)

nodes.name: IF: Is OTHER
nodes.notes: Routes OTHER emails. TRUE output connects to Reply Composer (Other).

nodes.name: IF: Is URGENT
nodes.notes: Routes URGENT emails. TRUE output connects to Escalation Check.

nodes.name: IF: Is AD
nodes.notes: Routes AD emails. TRUE output connects to Ad → Dashboard.

nodes.name: IF: Is SPAM
nodes.notes: Routes SPAM emails. TRUE output connects to Spam → Dashboard.

parameters.jsCode:
const c = ($json.classification || 'OTHER').toUpperCase();
return { json: { ...$json, _route: c } };

nodes.name: Route Tag
nodes.notes: Tags each email with _route field. Feeds 4 parallel IF nodes for routing.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, email_id: $json.email_id, sender: $json.sender, status: $json.status, reason: $json.reason, timestamp: $json.timestamp }) }}

nodes.name: Respond: Unsubscribe
nodes.notes: Returns unsubscribe result to dashboard.

nodes.name: Unsubscribe → Dashboard
nodes.notes: LIVE: Reports unsubscribe result to dashboard via https://siteware-email-dashboard.vercel.app/api/email/unsubscribe

nodes.name: Unsubscribe Not Possible
nodes.notes: No unsubscribe method available. Reports failure to dashboard.

nodes.name: Attempt Unsubscribe
nodes.notes: PRODUCTION: One-click unsubscribe via httpRequest (n8n Cloud compatible). Mailto flagged to audit log + dashboard. fetch() replaced with this.helpers.httpRequest().

nodes.name: Can Unsubscribe?
nodes.notes: TRUE = unsubscribe method available, attempt it. FALSE = no method found, report failure.

nodes.name: Detect Unsubscribe Method
nodes.notes: Determines which unsubscribe method is available: one-click URL, mailto, or none.

nodes.name: Webhook: Unsubscribe
nodes.notes: Dashboard sends unsubscribe request. Expects: email_id, sender_email, list_unsubscribe_url, list_unsubscribe_mailto

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, email_id: $json.email_id, old_classification: $json.old_classification, new_classification: $json.new_classification, timestamp: $json.retriage_completed_at }) }}

nodes.name: Respond: Re-Triaged
nodes.notes: Returns new classification to dashboard.

nodes.name: Re-Triage → Dashboard
nodes.notes: LIVE: Posts re-triage result to dashboard via https://siteware-email-dashboard.vercel.app.

nodes.name: Process Re-Triage Result
nodes.notes: Parses new classification from Siteware API. Compares old vs new classification for audit.

nodes.name: Re-Triage LLM
nodes.notes: Calls Siteware API to re-classify. Tells the AI the previous classification was wrong so it reconsiders.

parameters.jsCode:
const _raw = $input.first().json;
const input = _raw.body || _raw;

// Rebuild the email object in the same shape as Email Parser output
// so it can re-enter the triage pipeline
return [{ json: {
  email_id: input.email_id,
  sender_name: input.sender_name,
  sender_email: input.sender_email,
  sender_domain: input.sender_domain || input.sender_email.split('@')[1],
  subject: input.subject,
  body_plain: input.body_plain,
  body_html: input.body_html || '',
  has_attachments: input.has_attachments || false,
  attachment_names: input.attachment_names || [],
  detected_language_hint: input.detected_language || input.reply_language || 'de',
  is_thread: input.is_thread || false,
  thread_id: input.thread_id || null,
  list_unsubscribe_url: input.list_unsubscribe_url || null,
  list_unsubscribe_mailto: input.list_unsubscribe_mailto || null,
  received_at: input.received_at || input.date || new Date().toISOString(),
  raw_headers: input.raw_headers || {},
  _retriage: true,
  _original_classification: input.original_classification || 'unknown',
  _retriage_requested_at: new Date().toISOString()
}}];

nodes.name: Rebuild Email Object
nodes.notes: Reconstructs email in the standard Email Parser shape so it can re-enter triage. Flags _retriage: true for audit trail.

nodes.name: Webhook: Re-Triage
nodes.notes: Dashboard sends email back for re-classification. Expects: email_id, sender_name, sender_email, sender_domain, subject, body_plain, has_attachments, original_classification

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, email_id: $json.email_id, action: 'draft_rejected', timestamp: $json.rejected_at }) }}

nodes.name: Respond: Rejected
nodes.notes: Returns success response to dashboard confirming draft was rejected.

nodes.name: Log Rejection
nodes.notes: PRODUCTION: Persists rejection to Vercel /api/email/audit endpoint. Non-fatal — execution continues even if audit POST fails.

nodes.name: Webhook: Reject
nodes.notes: Receives rejected draft from dashboard. Expects JSON body with: email_id, reason (optional)

nodes.name: Respond: Sent
nodes.notes: Returns success response to dashboard confirming email was sent and archived.

nodes.name: Auto-Archive
nodes.notes: POSTs to Vercel /api/email/archive — fetches tenant IMAP creds from Turso and moves email to Archive folder. continueOnFail=true so send is not blocked if archive fails.

nodes.name: Send Email (SMTP)
nodes.notes: POSTs to Vercel /api/email/send — fetches tenant SMTP creds from Turso and sends via nodemailer. No hardcoded credentials.

nodes.name: Prepare Final Email
nodes.notes: Strips [ENTWURF] marker, appends signature, passes tenant_id through.

nodes.name: Webhook: Approve
nodes.notes: Receives approved draft from dashboard. Expects JSON body with: email_id, sender_email, subject, draft_html, draft_plain, reply_language

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), email_id: $json.email_id, node: 'reply_composer', classification: 'OTHER', action: 'draft_created', confidence: $json.confidence, error: null }}];

nodes.name: Log Other
nodes.notes: Audit log entry for OTHER draft creation.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({
  tenant_id: $json.tenant_id || "default",
  workflow: $json.workflow,
  category: $json.category,
  email_id: $json.email_id,
  sender_name: $json.sender_name,
  sender_email: $json.sender_email,
  subject: $json.subject,
  original_subject: $json.original_subject,
  original_preview: $json.original_preview,
  draft_html: $json.draft_html || "",
  draft_plain: $json.draft_plain || "",
  placeholders: $json.placeholders || [],
  reply_language: $json.reply_language || "de",
  confidence: Number($json.confidence ?? 0.5),
  review_reason: $json.review_reason || "",
  requires_human_review: Boolean($json.requires_human_review ?? true),
  low_confidence: Boolean($json.low_confidence),
  is_escalated: Boolean($json.is_escalated),
  sentiment_score: Number($json.sentiment_score ?? 0),
  date: $json.date,
  timestamp: $json.timestamp || new Date().toISOString()
}) }}

nodes.name: Other Draft → Dashboard
nodes.notes: POST to dashboard Express server via ngrok. Data pre-flattened by Prepare Other Draft node.

nodes.name: Prepare Other Draft
nodes.notes: Parses Reply Composer response for OTHER emails. Same logic as Urgent but without escalation flag.

nodes.name: Reply Composer (Other)
nodes.notes: Calls Siteware API with Reply Composer agent for non-urgent emails.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), email_id: $json.email_id, node: 'reply_composer', classification: 'URGENT', action: 'draft_created', confidence: $json.confidence, error: null }}];

nodes.name: Log Urgent
nodes.notes: Audit log entry for urgent draft creation.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({
  tenant_id: $json.tenant_id || "default",
  workflow: $json.workflow,
  category: $json.category,
  email_id: $json.email_id,
  sender_name: $json.sender_name,
  sender_email: $json.sender_email,
  subject: $json.subject,
  original_subject: $json.original_subject,
  original_preview: $json.original_preview,
  draft_html: $json.draft_html || "",
  draft_plain: $json.draft_plain || "",
  placeholders: $json.placeholders || [],
  reply_language: $json.reply_language || "de",
  confidence: Number($json.confidence ?? 0.5),
  review_reason: $json.review_reason || "",
  requires_human_review: Boolean($json.requires_human_review ?? true),
  low_confidence: Boolean($json.low_confidence),
  is_escalated: Boolean($json.is_escalated),
  sentiment_score: Number($json.sentiment_score ?? 0),
  date: $json.date,
  timestamp: $json.timestamp || new Date().toISOString()
}) }}

nodes.name: Urgent Draft → Dashboard
nodes.notes: POST to dashboard Express server via ngrok. Data pre-flattened by Prepare Urgent Draft node.

nodes.name: Prepare Urgent Draft
nodes.notes: Parses Reply Composer response from Siteware API. Extracts draft HTML/plain, placeholders, language. Preserves historyId.

nodes.name: Reply Composer (Urgent)
nodes.notes: Calls Siteware API: POST /v1/api/completion/{agentId} with Reply Composer agent. Drafts reply for URGENT emails. Same referenceId as triage for traceability.

nodes.name: SMS Notification
nodes.notes: NEEDS REPLACEMENT: Waiting on Andreas — Siteware SMS/voice API endpoint or Twilio credentials. Message payload is production-ready.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({
  tenant_id: $json.tenant_id || "default",
  workflow: "email_inbox",
  category: "ESCALATION",
  email_id: $json.email_id,
  sender_name: $json.sender_name,
  sender_email: $json.sender_email,
  subject: $json.original_subject || $json.subject,
  sentiment_score: Number($json.sentiment_score ?? 0),
  urgency: Number($json.urgency ?? 4),
  complaint_risk: Boolean($json.complaint_risk),
  legal_threat: Boolean($json.legal_threat),
  churn_risk: String($json.churn_risk ?? "medium"),
  summary: String($json.review_reason || "Escalation triggered"),
  body_plain: $json.body_plain || $json.original_preview || "",
  preview: $json.original_preview || "",
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  draft_html: $json.draft_html || "",
  draft_plain: $json.draft_plain || "",
  draft_subject: $json.subject,
  placeholders: $json.placeholders || []
}) }}

nodes.name: Escalation → Dashboard
nodes.notes: POST to dashboard Express server via ngrok. Sentiment fields extracted from nested $json.sentiment object.

nodes.name: Escalation Check
nodes.notes: Checks if URGENT email triggers escalation: sentiment < -0.5, urgency >= 4, complaint risk, legal threat, or high churn risk. ANY match = escalate.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), email_id: $json.email_id, node: 'triage', classification: 'AD', action: 'classified_ad', confidence: $json.confidence, error: null }}];

nodes.name: Log Ad
nodes.notes: Audit log entry for ad classification.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({
  tenant_id: $json.tenant_id || "default",
  workflow: "email_inbox",
  category: "AD",
  email_id: $json.email_id,
  sender_name: $json.sender_name,
  sender_email: $json.sender_email,
  sender_domain: $json.sender_domain,
  subject: $json.subject,
  preview: String($json.body_plain ?? "").substring(0, 150),
  date: $json.received_at,
  confidence: Number($json.confidence),
  low_confidence: Boolean($json.low_confidence),
  reasoning: $json.reasoning,
  list_unsubscribe_url: $json.list_unsubscribe_url || null,
  list_unsubscribe_mailto: $json.list_unsubscribe_mailto || null,
  unsubscribe_available: Boolean($json.list_unsubscribe_url || $json.list_unsubscribe_mailto)
}) }}

nodes.name: Ad → Dashboard
nodes.notes: POST to dashboard Express server via ngrok. No auth needed.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), email_id: $json.email_id, node: 'triage', classification: 'SPAM', action: 'classified_spam', confidence: $json.confidence, error: null }}];

nodes.name: Log Spam
nodes.notes: Audit log entry for spam classification.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ ({
  tenant_id: $json.tenant_id || "default",
  workflow: "email_inbox",
  category: "SPAM",
  email_id: $json.email_id,
  sender_name: $json.sender_name,
  sender_email: $json.sender_email,
  sender_domain: $json.sender_domain,
  subject: $json.subject,
  preview: String($json.body_plain ?? "").substring(0, 150),
  date: $json.received_at,
  confidence: Number($json.confidence),
  low_confidence: Boolean($json.low_confidence),
  reasoning: $json.reasoning,
  list_unsubscribe_url: null,
  list_unsubscribe_mailto: null,
  unsubscribe_available: false
}) }}

nodes.name: Spam → Dashboard
nodes.notes: POST to dashboard Express server via ngrok. No auth needed.

nodes.name: Confidence Gate
nodes.notes: Safety net. Low confidence → OTHER. Low-confidence SPAM → AD. High urgency OTHER → URGENT. Implements 'when uncertain, prefer safer option' rules.

nodes.name: Merge LLM Response
nodes.notes: Parses Siteware API response. The 'answer' field contains JSON string with classification + sentiment. Merges with original email data. Preserves historyId for conversation continuity.

nodes.name: Triage + Sentiment LLM
nodes.notes: Calls Siteware API: POST /v1/api/completion/{agentId}. Sends email content as prompt to the Triage+Sentiment agent. Returns classification + sentiment JSON in the 'answer' field. Uses referenceId for dashboard traceability.

nodes.name: Skip LLM?
nodes.notes: If Pre-Triage already classified with high confidence, skip the AI call. TRUE = skip (go to Confidence Gate). FALSE = call Siteware AI.

nodes.name: Pre-Triage Filter
nodes.notes: NEEDS DATA: VIP list empty — populate from CRM/onboarding. Spam blocklist is placeholder — replace with real domains. Both should be dynamic (loaded from SiteCore or n8n static data at runtime).

nodes.name: Email Parser
nodes.notes: Normalizes raw IMAP data into clean structured object. Extracts sender, body, unsub headers, thread info, language hint.
